{{Quote|Recently arrived from Old Mankoo, out by the Outer Rim. ''[…]'' Her planet's impoverished—although ''she'' seems to live well enough.|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], on Princess Tsian|<ref name="Republic 47" />}}
'''Old Mankoo''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Republic 47" /> located in the [[Old Mankoo system/Legends|Old Mankoo system]], a part of the [[Quess sector/Legends|Quess sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet was impoverished,<ref name="CSWE OldMankoo" /> and by [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Republic: Honor and Duty|''Honor and Duty'']] story arc of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series to twenty-three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[royal family of Old Mankoo]] had been overthrown.<ref name="Republic 48">[[Republic 48|''Republic'' 48]]</ref>

Although the<ref name="Republic 48" /> [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="Republic 47" /> [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Tsian]], a part of the [[Noble/Legends|royal family]], continued to live well, due to the [[Politics/Legends|political]] situation on her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the [[Gender/Legends|princess]] no longer had access to the funds she previously had. As a result, Tsian became an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] for hire. She had the [[Psicom 1260 interface]],<ref name="Republic 48" /> which allowed her to control [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]] with her thoughts,<ref name="Republic 47" /> [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetically]] implanted into her [[Brain/Legends|brain]]<ref name="Republic 48" /> and then used her [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] status as a princess to carry out political assassinations off-world.<ref name="Republic 47" />

==Behind the scenes==
Old Mankoo was first mentioned in [[Republic 47|''Honor and Duty, Part 2'']], the forty-seventh issue of the [[Dark Horse Comics]]' ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series. The issue was written by [[John Ostrander]]<ref name="Republic 47" /> and published on [[December 11]], [[2002]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54495/star_wars_republic_2002_47|text=Star Wars: Republic (2002) #47}}</ref>

The entry for Old Mankoo in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' placed the planet in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="CSWE OldMankoo" /> contradicting ''Republic 47'', which placed it in that region's vicinity instead.<ref name="Republic 47" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Old Mankoo [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square N-17 as part of the Mid Rim,<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to that book later specified that the Old Mankoo system was situated in the Quess [[Sector/Legends|sector]], which formed a part of the Mid Rim's border with the Outer Rim Territories.<ref name="Mid Rim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-sectors-of-the-mid-rim|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Sectors of the Mid Rim}}</ref>
